calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc. To Strike Or Tax Costs; Declaration Of Brandon R. Bell
Off Calendar Apr-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to strike or tax costs is granted as to the $150 jury fees deposited and is otherwise denied.

"Code of Civil Procedure section 1033 enumerates allowable costs and costs which are not allowable, and restricts allowable costs to those reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation." (Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 129.) "To recover a cost, it must be reasonably necessary to the litigation and reasonable in amount." (Thon v. Thompson (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1546, 1548.) Generally, if a cost item appears proper on its face, the burden is on the challenging party to prove it is not properly chargeable or is unreasonable. (Nelson, supra, 72 Cal.App.4th, at p. 131.) But "if the items are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774.) Ultimately, whether a cost item was reasonably necessary is a question of fact, but the court has no authority to award costs that are not authorized by statute. (Id.)

Jury fees are refundable unless jury trial is waived or continued with insufficient time to notify the jury that the trial will not proceed. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 631.3, subd. (a).) Here, trial was set for August 10, 2026. Defendant's motion is otherwise denied because the costs appear proper on their face and defendant does not show they are not properly chargeable.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
